ead.archdesc:
======= RMM04047Olin Sewall Pettingill, Jr. papers4047Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20132B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
lifesThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:46:41 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Olin Sewall Pettingill, Jr. papers
Pettingill, Olin Sewall, Jr., 1907-
4047
13.4 cubic feet. 13.4 cubic feet.
[ca. 1895-1990]. Correspondence, photographs, research material. Professional correspondence; photographic negatives of birds; notes and rsearch materials relating to his book. Collection material in English 2070619
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
May 2013
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, May 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, June 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Olin Sewall Pettingill was born in Maine on October 30, 1907. He received a Ph.D. from Cornell University in 1933. He was a college instructor of biology and ornithology and in 1960 was appointed Director of the Laboratory of Ornithology at Cornell University, a post he held until his retirement in 1973. For 35 summers, beginning in 1938, he taught ornithology at the University of Michigan Biological Station. He was secretary, vice-president, and president of the Wilson Ornithological Society and secretary of the American Ornithologists' Union, of which he was elected a life fellow in 1947. He was elected a Director of the National Audubon Society in 1955. He edited several books and magazines on ornithology. In 1974 he received the Arthur A. Allen Award from Cornell University for distinguished service to ornithology.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Professional correspondence; photographic negatives coded by species of bird; notes of places to see birds in each of the states in preparation for his book; some manuscript material; correspondence about the book THE BIRD WATCHER'S AMERICA, edited by Pettingill; vita, 1990; bibliography; and articles. Special projects include field work in the Galapagos Islands, Grand Manan, and Mexico, and research on the whooping crane. Major correspondents include W.J. Breckinridge, Maurice Brooks, Thornton W. Burgess, John Henry Dick, Harvey I. Fisher, Alfred O. Gross, George Hall, Margaret Morse Nice, Roger Tory Peterson, Maurice Rumboll, George M. Sutton (relating to a memorial), Gustav A. Swanson, and William Zimmerman.
Cite As:
Olin Sewall Pettingill, Jr. papers, #4047. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Negatives. College teachers. Ornithologists. Whooping crane. Scientific expeditions -- Mexico. Scientific expeditions -- Galapagos Islands. Scientific expeditions -- Argentina. Birds -- Research. Ornithology -- Field work. Ornithology -- Study and teaching. Pettingill, Olin Sewall, Jr. (Title of work: The bird watcher\'s America..) Breckinridge, W. J. Brooks, Maurice. Burgess, Thornton W. (Thornton Waldo), 1874-1965. Dick, John Henry, 1919- Fisher, Harvey I. Gross, Alfred Otto, 1883-1970. Hall, George. Nice, Margaret Morse, 1993-1974. Peterson, Roger Tory, 1908- Rumboll, Maurice. Sutton, George Miksch, 1898- Swanson, Gustav Adolf, 1910- Zimmerman, William.
